description: Master table of all SAP B1 system users. Every document has a UserSign field referencing this table — used for audit trails, productivity reports, and access-level analysis.

joins.purpose: Get who created each AR invoice
Get who created each AP invoice
Get who posted each journal entry

columns.description: Unique numeric user ID. PK and FK target in all documents via UserSign.
User's login name used to sign in to SAP B1.
User's full display name.
User's email address.
User's office phone or extension.
Department the user belongs to. FK to OUDP.
Y if user account is locked / deactivated.
Y if this user has full system administrator rights.
Default branch this user operates from.
User's mobile number.

description: Defines all company departments. Referenced by OHEM (employees) and OUSR (users). Needed to make department-level HR and cost reports meaningful — OHEM stores only a numeric dept code without this lookup.

joins.purpose: Get all employees in this department
Get all users in this department

columns.description: Unique department code. PK and FK target in OHEM and OUSR.
Full department name (e.g. Sales, Finance, Operations, Procurement).
Free-text notes about this department.

description: Defines company branches/locations for HR purposes. Referenced by OHEM.branch to assign employees to physical locations. Different from OBPL (which is for GST registration) — this is purely for HR org structure.

joins.purpose: Get all employees at this branch

columns.description: Unique HR branch code. PK and FK in OHEM.
Full branch name (e.g. Mumbai HO, Delhi Branch, Chennai Plant).
Notes about this branch.

description: Defines budget scenarios (e.g. FY2025 Budget, FY2025 Revised). Each scenario holds a complete set of budget lines in BGT1. Enables budget vs actual analysis across the GL.

joins.purpose: Get all GL account budget amounts for this scenario

columns.description: Unique internal ID for this budget scenario. PK. FK target in BGT1.
Name of the budget (e.g. Annual Budget 2025, Revised Budget Q3).
Financial year this budget applies to.
Y if opening balance is included in budget calculations.
Description or notes about this budget scenario.

business_name: Budget Lines (GL Account Budget Amounts per Period)
description: Stores budgeted amounts per GL account per period per budget scenario. The primary table for budget vs actual reporting. Join with OACT for account names and with JDT1 actuals for variance analysis.

joins.purpose: Get budget scenario name and fiscal year
Get GL account name for budget vs actual reports

columns.description: FK to OBGT.AbsId. Composite PK.
FK to OACT.AcctCode. Composite PK.
Distribution dimension (cost centre). Composite PK.
Budgeted amount for January.
Budgeted amount for February.
Budgeted amount for March.
Budgeted amount for April.
Budgeted amount for May.
Budgeted amount for June.
Budgeted amount for July.
Budgeted amount for August.
Budgeted amount for September.
Budgeted amount for October.
Budgeted amount for November.
Budgeted amount for December.
Sum of all monthly budget amounts for this account.

description: Header for each customer service call — complaint, repair request, AMC visit, or technical support. Tracks status, priority, SLA, assigned technician, and resolution. Key for service analytics: TAT, SLA breach, technician productivity.

joins.purpose: Get work done, parts used, time spent on this service call
Get customer name and contact details for service call
Get technician assigned to this service call
Get the product/item the service call is about

columns.description: Unique system key for this service call. FK target in SCL1.
User-facing sequential service call number.
Customer who raised this service call. FK to OCRD.
Customer name for this service call.
Item the service call is raised for. FK to OITM.
Current status of the service call.
Urgency level of this service call.
Type of service — complaint, repair, preventive, installation.
Date the service call was registered.
Date the service call was resolved and closed.
Employee assigned to resolve this call. FK to OHEM.
Serial number of the specific unit under service. FK to OSRN.
Description of the issue/complaint reported by customer.
How the issue was resolved.

business_name: Service Call Activity Lines (Work Done / Parts Used)
description: Each row is one activity logged against a service call — technician visit, part replaced, time spent. Used for service cost analysis and SLA tracking.

joins.purpose: Get service call header — customer, item, status
Get spare part details used in service

columns.description: FK to OSCL.DocEntry.
Sequential line number.
Type of activity logged.
Part used during service. FK to OITM.
Quantity of spare part or hours of labour.
Cost per unit or per hour.
Total cost of this activity (Qty × Price).
Date this activity was performed.
Notes on what was done during this activity.

description: Tracks sales opportunities / deals in the CRM pipeline. Each row is one potential deal — customer, estimated value, stage, close date, probability. Foundation for sales pipeline and forecast reports.

joins.purpose: Get products/services in this opportunity
Get the prospect or customer for this opportunity
Get the salesperson owning this opportunity

columns.description: Unique system key for this opportunity. PK. FK in OPR1.
Name or title of this sales opportunity.
Lead or customer this opportunity is with. FK to OCRD.
Name of the prospect or customer.
Sales rep responsible for this opportunity. FK to OSLP.
Current status of the sales opportunity.
Current stage in the sales pipeline (numeric ID of the stage).
Date the deal is expected to be won or lost.
Date this opportunity was created.
Maximum potential revenue from this deal.
MaxSumLoc × Probability — used in weighted pipeline reports.
Estimated probability of winning this deal (0–100).
Industry or market segment of the prospect.
Free-text notes on this opportunity.

description: Products and services included in a sales opportunity. Each line is one item the prospect might buy. Used to forecast product-level demand from the pipeline.

joins.purpose: Get opportunity header — customer, stage, probability
Get product/item name and group

columns.description: FK to OOPR.OpprId. Composite PK with Line.
Sequential line number.
Product being proposed. FK to OITM.
Description of the product or service.
Expected quantity the prospect will buy.
Price being quoted for this product.
Estimated deal value for this product line.

description: Defines payment term codes used across business partners and documents (e.g. Net 30, Net 60, Immediate, 2% 10 Net 30). Controls due date calculation on invoices and receipts.

joins.purpose: Get all BPs with this payment term

columns.description: Unique payment term code. PK.
Display name (e.g. Net 30, Immediate, 50% Advance).
Additional months added to invoice date for due date.
Additional days added for due date calculation.
Discount percentage if paid within discount days.
Number of days from invoice date within which discount applies.
How the due date is calculated.

description: Customer or vendor-specific price overrides for individual items. Overrides the standard price list (OPLN/ITM1) for a specific business partner. Enables contract pricing, special deals, and loyalty discounts.

joins.purpose: Get customer/vendor for whom the special price applies
Get item name for the special price

columns.description: Customer/vendor this special price is for. Composite PK. FK to OCRD.
Item with special price. Composite PK. FK to OITM.
Agreed special price for this customer-item combination.
Currency of the special price.
Discount % from the standard price list (alternative to fixed price).
Date from which this special price is effective.
Date until which this special price is valid.

description: Defines payment methods used in outgoing and incoming payments — NEFT, RTGS, Cheque, Cash, UPI, DD. Referenced by OVPM and ORCT. Essential for payment mode analysis and bank reconciliation.

joins.purpose: Get all vendor payments made via this method
Get all receipts received via this method

columns.description: Unique code for payment method. PK.
Full name (e.g. NEFT, RTGS, Bank Transfer, UPI, Cash, Cheque, DD).
Whether this method is for outgoing or incoming payments.
Default bank account to use with this payment method.
Default bank for this payment method.

description: Lookup table for all countries. Referenced in business partner addresses (CRD1), company details, and documents with country fields. Required for geographic reporting and import/export document generation.

joins.purpose: Get all BPs in this country
Get country name for BP ship-to/bill-to addresses

columns.description: ISO 2-letter country code (e.g. IN, US, AE, GB). PK.
Full country name (e.g. India, United States, UAE).
ISO 3-letter country code (e.g. IND, USA, ARE).

description: Defines shipping methods and carriers (Road, Rail, Air, Sea, Courier). Referenced on delivery notes and sales orders via ShipType. Used for logistics and freight cost analysis.

joins.purpose: Get all deliveries shipped via this carrier/method
Get all invoices dispatched via this method

columns.description: Unique code for the shipping method. PK. FK in ODLN, OINV.
Name of the shipping method (e.g. FedEx, DHL, Road Transport, Indian Post).
Website URL for the carrier (for tracking links).

description: Stores documents that are awaiting approval by a manager or approver. Each row is one pending approval request. Used for workflow monitoring — 'show all purchase orders waiting for approval', SLA on approval turnaround.

joins.purpose: Get the user who submitted the document for approval
Get the approver assigned to review this request

columns.description: Unique ID for this approval request. PK.
Type of document awaiting approval (e.g. 22=PO, 18=AP Invoice).
DocEntry of the document awaiting approval.
User who created the document and submitted for approval. FK to OUSR.
User assigned to approve this request. FK to OUSR.
Current status of the approval request.
Date the approval request was created.
Date the approver took action.
Total value of the document awaiting approval.
Notes or reason from the approver (especially on rejection).

business_name: Down Payment Invoice — AR (Advance Receipt from Customer)
description: Records advance payments / down payment requests raised on customers before goods are delivered. Linked to a final A/R invoice when the deal closes. Common in project-based and high-value sales.

joins.purpose: Get line details of the advance payment invoice
Get customer who paid the advance
Get the final invoice this advance was applied against

columns.description: Unique system key. FK target in DPI1.
User-facing sequential DP invoice number.
Customer from whom advance is received. FK to OCRD.
Customer name at time of advance.
Date of the advance payment invoice.
Total advance received from customer.
Status of this down payment.
GL posting for this advance. FK to OJDT.

description: Line items on an AR down payment invoice — items or services for which advance has been received.

joins.purpose: Get advance invoice header
Get item details

columns.description: FK to ODPI.DocEntry.
Sequential line number.
FK to OITM.
Quantity on this advance line.
Price per unit.
Total advance amount on this line.

business_name: Down Payment Request — AP (Advance to Vendor)
description: Records advance payments made to vendors before goods or services are received. Applied against the final AP invoice when the vendor delivers. Common for imports, project procurement, and capital goods purchases.

joins.purpose: Get lines of the vendor advance payment request
Get vendor receiving the advance

columns.description: Unique system key. FK target in DPO1.
User-facing sequential number.
Vendor receiving advance payment. FK to OCRD.
Vendor name at time of advance.
Date of this vendor advance payment.
Total advance paid to vendor.
Status of this advance.

description: Line items on an AP down payment — items or services for which advance has been paid to the vendor.

joins.purpose: Get AP advance header details
Get item details

columns.description: FK to ODPO.DocEntry.
Sequential line number.
FK to OITM.
Quantity on this advance line.
Price per unit.
Total advance on this line.

description: Master record for all company fixed assets — machinery, vehicles, computers, furniture. Tracks acquisition cost, depreciation method, accumulated depreciation, and net book value. Required for asset register and depreciation reports.

joins.purpose: Get item description for this fixed asset
Get location/branch where asset is situated

columns.description: Unique asset code. PK. Links to OITM for the asset item.
Classification of the asset (e.g. Plant, Vehicle, Furniture, IT).
Date the asset was capitalised / put into service.
Date the asset was purchased.
Original cost of the asset when acquired.
Current written-down value after accumulated depreciation.
Total depreciation charged to date on this asset.
Expected scrap value at end of useful life.
Expected useful life in months for depreciation calculation.
Method used to depreciate this asset.
Current status of the asset.
Where this asset is physically located. FK to OWHS.
Manufacturer or company serial number of this asset.

description: Records imported bank statements for bank reconciliation. Each row is one bank statement for one period. Lines stored in BNK1. Used to reconcile book balance against bank balance and identify uncleared transactions.

joins.purpose: Get individual bank transactions in this statement

columns.description: Unique system key. PK. FK target in BNK1.
Bank for this statement.
GL account for this bank. FK to OACT.
Bank account opening balance for this statement period.
Bank account closing balance per statement.
Date this bank statement was generated.

description: Individual transaction lines from an imported bank statement. Each row is one credit or debit on the bank account. Matched against SAP payment/receipt records during bank reconciliation.

joins.purpose: Get the bank statement this transaction belongs to

columns.description: FK to OBNK.AbsEntry. Composite PK with LineNum.
Sequential line number.
Date of the bank transaction.
Bank reference / UTR number / cheque number.
Amount credited to the bank account (receipt).
Amount debited from the bank account (payment).
Running balance after this transaction.
Description from the bank statement (e.g. NEFT from XYZ Ltd).
Y if this line has been matched to a SAP payment/receipt record.
External reference (UTR, IMPS ref, RTGS ref) for matching.
